PM2.5: 10 ug/M or lower and PM10: 2.5 ug/m or lower.

M01.explanation:

different scenarios in which these thresholds should be benchmarked against that 
take into consideration what spaces are contained within the project and the 
pollution levels of the region.

M01.stem: In Feature A01: Air Quality, what is the acceptable threshold for particulate matter in commercial kitchens?

In a project with regularly occupied spaces at or below grade, where should the radon test be performed for Feature A01: Air Quality?

At an existing office building campus, signage indicating an outdoor smoking ban is posted at all building entrances. The signs indicate the ban on smoking within 7.5 m (25 ft) of all building entrances, operable windows, building air intakes and regularly occupied exterior building spaces. What signage must be added to the courtyard areas more than 7.5 m (25 ft) from building entrances to meet the requirements of Part 2: Prohibit Outdoor Smoking of Feature A02: Smoke-Free Environment?

options.text: The local municipal ordinance on smoking in exterior public areas, posted every 15 m (50 ft) along the edge of the no smoking area.
Contact information for a smoking cessation program, posted every 15 m (50 ft) along the edge of the no smoking area.
The location of designated smoking areas, posted every 30 m (100 ft) along walkways.
The hazards of smoking, posted at ground level every 30 m (100 ft) along walkways.

Which of the following features from the Air concept is a precondition for WELL Certification?

According to Part 2: Prohibit Outdoor Smoking of Feature A02: Smoke-Free Environment, there must be signage at all outdoor areas in the project's boundary. What is the verification method required for this feature?
What is the maximum level of carbon monoxide for all spaces in Part 3: Meet Threshold for Inorganic Gases of Feature A05: Enhanced Air Quality?
In mechanically ventilated spaces, ventilation systems are designed to meet supply and exhaust rates set in one or more of the following verification guidelines:
M01.explanation: Part 1: Ensure Adequate Ventilation of Feature A03: Ventilation Design.

M01.stem: In naturally ventilated spaces with no mechanical ventilation, what requirements must be met for at least 90% of the project?
M01.explanation: Part 1: Ensure Adequate Ventilation of Feature A03: Ventilation Design.

M01.stem: The use of UVGI is required for which of the following features?

options.text: Feature A14: Microbe and Mold Control

M01.stem: Which of the following is NOT a part of Feature A04: Construction Pollution Management?
What are the minimum dimensions required for entryway walk-off systems?

options.text: At least the width of the doorway and 2m (6ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least 2m (6ft) wide and 3m (10ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least the width of the doorway and 3m (10ft) long in the direction of travel.
At least 2m (6ft) wide and 4m (12ft) long in the direction of travel.

M01.explanation: Part 1: Design Healthy Entryways of Feature A09: Pollution Infiltration Management.

M01.stem: An office space is being constructed on the 5th floor of a downtown tower. According to Feature A03: Ventilation Design, when is the ideal time to perform testing and balancing on the HVAC system in this project?
In Part 2: Meet Enhanced Thresholds for Organic Gases of Feature A05: Enhanced Air Quality, which thresholds are to be met?

options.text: Formaldehyde 9 ug/m3 or lower & Toluene 300 ug/m3 or lower.
Formaldehyde 9ug/m3 or lower & PM 2.5 12 ug/m3 or lower.
PM 2.5 12 ug/m3 or lower & Toluene 300 ug/m3 or lower.

M01.stem: For naturally ventilated spaces, it is necessary to ensure that adequate outdoor air quality & _____________ are met.
Since it is difficult to test for every potential pollutant in the indoor air of a building, what easily detectable pollutant serves as a proxy for other indoor pollutants?
M01.explanation: Overview of Feature A06: Enhanced Ventilation Design.

M01.stem: In Tokyo, a new mixed-use complex is being planned to replace a retired railyard. The ambient air has a high concentration of small particulates. According to Feature A12: Air Filtration, what is the project team required to install in order to protect the building occupants from exposure to the particulates?

options.text: Adequate media filters based on the annual average PM 2.5 of the area.

M01.stem: A new office building in San Diego has an average annual PM 2.5 of 21 ug/m. What type of filter is required to meet Part 1: Implement Particle Filtration of Feature A12: Air Filtration?

A new medical office building is being constructed in a suburban campus setting. The 2-story building is planned to hold 6 tenants, and the main lobby will host a 24-hour security desk and a daily parking attendant. The owner would like to pursue WELL Certification for the building. Which of the following would qualify as an adequate building entry design?

options.text: 2 sets of normally-closed doors in a series: the main building doors, and the tenant suite doors.
2 sets of normally-closed doors in a series: the main building doors and the individual office doors.
3 sets of normally-closed doors in series: the main building doors, the first floor elevator door, and the second floor elevator door.
An entryway vestibule with 2 sets of normally-closed doors.

M01.stem: In an urban high-rise building, a contractor is preparing to begin construction on a 10,000 ft2 (930m2) tenant suite. The team is preparing to use a combination of strategies during construction to mitigate the risk of construction pollution to the tenant suite. Envelope protection and filter replacement are some of the strategies that can be used. What is another strategy that can be used during construction to reduce the risk of construction pollution?
M01.explanation: Overview of Feature A04: Construction Pollution Management.

M01.stem: Feature A09: Pollution Infiltration Management's intent is to minimize the introduction of pollutants into indoor air through the building envelope and at building entrances. One strategy is to design healthy entryways. What else can we do to minimize pollutants in our buildings?
For which Feature in the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™) is envelope commissioning required?
To earn points toward Part 1: Increase Outdoor Air Supply of Feature A06: Enhanced Ventilation Design, minimum fresh air supply rates should exceed ASHRAE by what amount?
What feature uses displacement ventilation to improve indoor air quality by delivering fresh outdoor air at floor level?
M01.explanation:
Feature A06: Enhanced Ventilation Design requires implementation of advanced 
ventilation strategies that can achieve higher air quality levels and thus benefit 
human health and productivity. One strategy is displacement ventilation which 
improves indoor air quality, by delivering fresh outdoor air at the floor level, leaving 
warmer polluted indoor air, such as CO2 to be extracted above the height of the 
ventilation zone.

M01.stem: Ensuring interior rooms that may contain sources of air pollution are isolated from adjacent spaces with self-closing doors is a criteria for which of the following WELL Features?

The Property Manager for a Johannesburg office building is installing lobby signage to display real-time data on indoor environmental quality. Which of the following is NOT a required parameter for this display to meet Feature A08: Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness requirements?
Which of the following are ongoing requirements for indoor air monitoring in Feature A08: Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness?

options.text: Record data at hourly intervals; Send monthly reports to IWBI.
Data is analyzed 24 hours and submitted monthly through WELL Online.
Data are analyzed for regularly occupied hours and submitted to annually through WELL Online.
Data are analyzed and submitted to IWBI.

M01.stem: A property management company is considering submitting its portfolio of 9 urban office properties for WELL Certification. The property teams are reviewing the requirements for air data record keeping. What should the property teams plan to provide in the WELL application for this feature?

options.text: A written policy for each property, including corrective action steps.
Results from flow hood testing that confirm compliance with ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation calculations.
On-site Photographs, Letter of Assurance – Engineer

M01.explanation: Feature A08: Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness.

M01.stem: For Feature A07: Operable Windows, what requirement must be met before integrating operable windows into a building design?

options.text: At least 75% of regularly occupied spaces have operable windows that provide access to outdoor air.
At least 25% of regularly occupied spaces have operable windows that provide access to outdoor air.
All windows in regularly occupied spaces have operable windows that provide access to outdoor air.
No windows in regularly occupied spaces have operable windows.

M01.stem: A project team in Montreal is designing a new hospital wing for administrative offices. During the schematic design phase, the team has an opportunity to plan for a data-gathering station to measure outdoor air parameters. Where should the team locate the devices to comply with Feature A07: Operable Windows?

options.text: Within 3 m (30ft) of the main building entrance.
Within 4 km (2.5 mi) of the building.
On the building exterior, halfway between the ground level and the roof.

M01.stem: Which of the following does NOT describe an advantage of displacement ventilation systems?

options.text: Air is not delivered and pushed through the return air path (often the dirtiest portion of the air stream).
The airflow tends to concentrate pollutants near the ceiling, out of the breathing zone.
Air is delivered at very low velocity, preventing occupant discomfort due to air speed.
Air can be delivered slightly cooler or warmer than the desired space temperature because registers are at raised floor height.

M01.stem: A developer is planning a new convention center, including over 30 conference rooms of all sizes. The team is interested in pursuing WELL Certification and is considering a displacement ventilation system for the conference rooms. In Part 2: Improve Ventilation Effectiveness of Feature A06: Enhanced Ventilation Design, what must be used as a basis for design for this system?

options.text: ASHRAE Guidelines RP-949 or REHVA Guidebook No. 1

M01.stem: What is the primary reason to introduce activated carbon filters to the main air ducts of an existing building?

options.text: To reduce VOCs in the indoor air.
To reduce HVAC noise from vibration.

M01.explanation: Overview for Feature A13: Enhanced Supply Air.

M01.stem: Ultraviolet Germicidal Irradiation (UVGI) is one method for treating recirculated air in Feature A13: Enhanced Air Supply. What is another approved treatment method according to this feature?
M01.explanation: Overview of Feature A13: Enhanced Supply Air.

M01.stem: In buildings that use forced-air cooling systems, which are strategies to implement air sanitation in Feature A14: Microbe and Mold Control?

options.text: Ultraviolet germicidal irradiation and carbon filters.

M01.stem: What regular documentation is required for WELL project teams to demonstrate that air purification strategies are in property operation for Feature A13: Enhanced Supply Air?

options.text: Evidence of filter maintenance is submitted annually through WELL Online.
Annual letters from third party inspectors that air filtration/sanitation systems are in place.
Monthly letters from third party inspectors that air filtration/sanitation systems are in place.

M01.stem: Under what circumstance should combustion-based space heaters be allowed in regularly occupied spaces?

options.text: When they have automatic shut-off timers with a maximum of 15 minute cycles.
Combustion-based space heaters should never be allowed in regularly occupied spaces.
When they are located within 7.5 m (25ft) of an exterior door.
When they are located on an exterior wall and directly vented to the outside.

M01.stem: Which substance has 210 times the binding affinity for hemoglobin compared to oxygen and is toxic to humans?

An office building is located where the annual average ambient PM 2.5 level is 35 ug/m3 or higher. What threshold must be met for the project to meet the standards for Feature A01: Fundamental Air Quality?

options.text: PM 2.5 equal to 60% of the 24- or 48- hour average of outdoor levels on days of performance testing.
PM 10 equal to 25% of the 24- or 48- hour average of outdoor levels on days of performance testing.
PM 2.5 equal to 10% of the 24- or 48- hour average of outdoor levels on days of performance testing.
PM 2.5 equal to 30% of the 24- or 48- hour average of outdoor levels on days of performance testing.

M01.stem: A new library would like to meet the standards for Feature A05: Enhanced Air Quality. What threshold must be met for organic gases to achieve this feature?

options.text: Formaldehyde less than 13 µg/m³ or lower
Formaldehyde less than 25 µg/m³ or lower
Formaldehyde less than 50 µg/m³ or lower

M01.stem: What is the intent of Feature A01: Air Quality?

options.text: To provide a basic understanding of indoor air pollutants.
To provide a humidity control panel to occupants.
To provide a basic level of indoor air quality that contributes to the health and well-being of building users.
To provide a basic understanding of the role the building envelope plays in indoor air quality.

M01.stem: Which feature is a precondition of the Air concept?

options.text: Feature A08: Air Quality Monitoring and Awareness

M01.stem: A nonprofit is renovating floors 4 through 6 of an office building. How will Part 4: Meet Thresholds for Radon of Feature A01: Air Quality impact this project?

options.text: The project will need to test for radon to ensure it is 0.15 Bq/L or lower.
This will not impact this project because this requirement is only for regularly occupied spaces at or below grade.
The project will need to have a professional test for radon to ensure it is 0.15 Bq/L or lower.
The project will need one test per 25,000 ft2 of regularly occupied space at or below grade.

M01.stem: In order to achieve the optimization, Feature A12: Air Filtration, what is the verification method for filter maintenance?

Feature A11: Source Separation preserves indoor air quality and maximizes olfactory comfort in occupied spaces through the isolation and proper ventilation of indoor air pollution sources and chemical storage areas. What requirements must be met for dwelling units, all ovens, cooking burners and stovetop cooking appliances that use a range hood?

options.text: Exhaust air is vented to the space between apartments.
Exhaust air outlet are separated from any air intakes by at least 3ft.
There is no requirement for exhaust air in dwelling units.
Exhaust air is vented directly to the outdoors.

M01.stem: To achieve Part 1: Implement Ultraviolet Air Treatment of Feature A14: Microbe and Mold Control, what are the two UV System areas the requirements are based on?

options.text: UV system design and UV system maintenance and inspection.
Humidity control monitor and UV system design.

M01.explanation:
The growth of microorganisms and mold can be prevented and mitigated through a 
combination of regular mold inspections and placement of microbe inactivation 
techniques, such as Ultraviolet Germicidal Irradiation (UVGI) systems.

M01.stem: An advertising firm in Albany wants to allow for operable windows in their offices. What do the indicator lights at windows need to cue occupants?

options.text: When conditions outside are not suitable for opening windows.
When conditions outside are suitable for opening windows.
When PM 2.5 is 50 ug/m3 or lower.
When relative humidity is 85% or lower.

M01.explanation: Part 2: Manage Window Use of Feature A07: Operable Windows.

The WELL Building Standard was pioneered by which group?

options.text: Feature C08.1 Offer New Parent Leave’

Who is responsible for completing a test after occupancy that confirms the project meets minimum air quality requirements?

How many preconditions are there that a project must meet?

Tobacco Cessation is a feature in which category?
How many WELL concept advisors participated in creating WELL v2?
How many possible points may a project pursue in the Innovation Concept?
How many concepts are there in WELL v2?
From the date of registration, how long do projects teams utilizing WELL have to complete documentation submission and schedule Performance Verification?

options.text: 5 years from the date of registration
3 years from the date of registration
2 years from the date of the on-site Performance Verification
3 years from the date of the on-site Performance Verification

M11.stem: Which of the following is NOT a type of licensed professional that can sign a Letter of Assurance for a WELL Feature?
Which of the following are types of annotated documents that must be turned in for a WELL certification application?

An existing building is ready to begin the WELL certification process. They have registered the project and are gathering project documentation. At what point should the project team schedule the Performance Verification?

options.text: After the Documentation Review phase is marked approved in WELL Online
Once the project has 12 months of 100% occupied data
As soon as the WELL Assessor has been assigned to the project

M11.stem: Which of the following conditions must be met for a new building project team to schedule the WELL Performance Verification?

options.text: 4 months since the spaces certificate of occupancy was issued and 50% occupancy achieved.
Documentation approved, at least one month since certificate of occupancy issued, and 50% occupancy achieved.
One month since the certificate of occupancy issued and commissioning complete.

M11.stem: If a project is so expansive that a single WELL Assessor cannot complete the Performance Verification in a reasonable amount of time, what alternative path is available to the WELL Assessors?

options.text: Add up to 1 additional WELL Assessor
Request 1 additional WELL Assessor for every 9,300 m2 (100,100 ft2) of project area
Employ a pre-qualified testing organization that the WELL Assessor oversees
Extend the time that the assigned WELL Assessor is scheduled to perform the Performance Verification

M11.stem: At which website should a project team register their WELL Building project?
What are the WELL certification levels a project can be awarded?
M11.explanation:
Projects must achieve all preconditions, as well as a certain number of points 
towards either a Bronze, Silver, Gold, or Platinum Certification.

M11.stem: All preconditions are required to be met for WELL v2. How many features is that?
After the WELL certification award, which of the following would a project team NOT be required to provide reports on to maintain its status as a WELL Certified project?

options.text: Proof of maintenance (e.g., logs of cleaning schedules and filter replacement).
Ongoing monitoring of environmental parameters (e.g., air and water quality).

M11.explanation:
In order to maintain status as a WELL Certified project, project teams must adhere to 
annual reporting requirements. Certain WELL features require projects to submit 
annual reports via WELL Online regarding the following: 
A. Results of occupancy surveys 
B. Proof of maintenance (e.g., logs of cleaning schedules and filter replacement) 
C. Ongoing monitoring of environmental parameters (e.g., air and water quality)

M11.stem: Which WELL concept requires that in addition to proper HVAC system design, mechanically ventilated projects perform regular system maintenance since inadequate maintenance is associated with reduced ventilation performance and a deterioration in indoor air quality and thermal conditions?
M11.explanation:
Feature A03 Ventilation Design of the WELL Air concept requires projects to bring in 
fresh air from the outside through mechanical and/or natural means in order to 
dilute human- and product-generated air pollutants. With proper HVAC system 
design, mechanically ventilated projects need to perform regular system 
maintenance, since inadequate maintenance is associated with reduced ventilation 
performance and a deterioration in indoor air quality and thermal conditions.

M11.stem: How many optimizations are available for a WELL v2 project?
M11.explanation:
There are 98 optimizations available, 24 preconditions are required, and there are 11 
concepts for WELL projects.

M11.stem: Which WELL concept includes a Feature that takes into account characteristics such as color rendering and flicker?
M11.explanation:
Feature L08 Electric Light Quality of the WELL Light concept requires projects to take 
into account characteristics of electric light used in the space, such as color rendering 
and flicker.

M11.stem: A project team for an existing building is considering the addition of artwork to the central stairway, which already includes view windows to the exterior. Which WELL concept includes a Feature that rewards combinations of windows and artwork like this?
M11.explanation:
In Part 1 - Design Aesthetic Staircases of Feature V03: Circulation Network within the 
WELL Movement concept, At least one staircase is open to regular occupants, 
services all floors of the project and is aesthetically designed through the inclusion of 
at least two independent strategies from the following list on each floor: 
a. Music 
b. Artwork 
c. Light levels of at least 215 lux when in use 
d. Windows or skylights that provide access to daylight 
e. Natural design elements (e.g., plants, water features, images of nature) 
f. Gamification

M11.stem: Which WELL concept promotes the implementation of design, policy and programmatic strategies that support cognitive and emotional health through a variety of prevention and treatment efforts?
M11.explanation:
The WELL Mind concept promotes implementation of design, policy and 
programmatic strategies that support cognitive and emotional health through a 
variety of prevention and treatment efforts. In combination, these interventions have 
the potential to positively impact the short- and long-term mental health and 
well-being of individuals of diverse backgrounds throughout a community.

M11.stem: At a non-profit with 200 employees, annual Occupant Indoor Environmental Quality surveys are administered to a representative sample of 30% of the employees. Which WELL Concept requires that the survey results are delivered to IWBI within 30 days?
M11.explanation:
Aggregate results from surveys within the WELL Community concept are reported 
within 30 days to the following groups: 
a. Building owners and managers. 
b. Building occupants (upon request). 
c. The International WELL Building Institute.

M11.stem: What WELL concept aims to reduce circadian phase disruption, improve sleep quality and positively impact mood, and productivity?
M11.explanation:
The WELL Light concept aims to provide a lighting environment that reduces 
circadian phase disruption, improves sleep quality and positively impacts mood and 
productivity.

M11.stem: Which of the following organizations is a public benefit corporation whose mission is to improve human health and well-being through the built environment?
M11.explanation:
International WELL Building Institute (IWBI) is a public benefit corporation whose 
mission is to improve human health and well-being through the built environment. 
Public benefit corporations like IWBI are an emerging U.S. structure for corporations 
committed to balancing public benefits with profitability – harnessing the power of 
private capital for the greater good.

M11.stem: IWBI was established pursuant to which of the following commitments?
M11.explanation:
IWBI was established by Delos in 2013 pursuant to a Clinton Global Initiative 
commitment to improve the way people live by developing spaces that enhance 
occupant health and quality of life by sharing the WELL Building Standard globally.

M11.stem: Projects must achieve all preconditions. How many more points must a project achieve to reach Silver Certification?
M11.explanation:
For WELL Bronze certification a project needs to achieve 40 total points, for a WELL 
Silver certification a project needs to achieve 50 total points, for a WELL Gold 
certification a project needs to achieve 60 total points, and for a WELL Platinum 
certification, a project needs to achieve 80 total points. From Bronze to Silver a 
project needs to achieve 10 more points.

M11.stem: What percent of WELL preconditions must be met in order to achieve Silver Certification?
M11.explanation:
Projects must achieve all preconditions, as well as a certain number of points 
towards different levels of WELL Certification.

M11.stem: Projects must achieve all preconditions, as well as a certain number of points towards different levels of WELL Certification. How many more points must a project achieve to reach Gold Certification?
M11.explanation:
For WELL Bronze certification a project needs to achieve 40 total points, for a WELL 
Silver certification a project needs to achieve 50 total points, for a WELL Gold 
certification a project needs to achieve 60 total points, and for a WELL Platinum 
certification a project needs to achieve 80 total points. From Bronze to Gold a project 
needs to achieve 20 more points.

M11.stem: Projects must achieve all preconditions, as well as a certain number of points towards different levels of WELL Certification. How many more points must a project achieve to reach Platinum Certification?
M11.explanation:
For WELL Bronze certification a project needs to achieve 40 total points, for a WELL 
Silver certification a project needs to achieve 50 total points, for a WELL Gold 
certification a project needs to achieve 60 total points, and for a WELL Platinum 
certification, a project needs to achieve 80 total points. From Bronze to Platinum a 
project needs to achieve 40 more points.

M11.stem: How many optimizations are required for WELL Bronze Certification?
M11.explanation:
Optimizations are optional pathways for projects to meet certification requirements 
in WELL. Project teams may select which optimizations to pursue and which parts to 
focus on within each optimization.

M11.stem: What are the minimum points per concept required for WELL Silver Certification?
M11.explanation:
The minimum points per concept for a WELL Bronze certification is zero, a WELL 
Silver certification is one, a WELL Gold certification is two, and a WELL Platinum 
certification is three.

M11.stem: What are the minimum points per concept required for WELL Gold Certification?
M11.explanation:
The minimum points per concept for a WELL Bronze certification is zero, a WELL 
Silver certification is one, a WELL Gold certification is two, and a WELL Platinum 
certification is three.

M11.stem: What are the minimum points per concept required for a WELL Platinum Certification?
M11.explanation:
The minimum points per concept for a WELL Bronze certification is zero, a WELL 
Silver certification is one, a WELL Gold certification is two, and a WELL Platinum 
certification is three.

M11.stem: What are the two main groups that WELL v2 projects fall into?
M11.explanation:
WELL v2 projects fall into one of two main groups, determined primarily by 
ownership type:

M11.stem: What is the WELL Portfolio?

options.text: A program that enables organizations to improve well-being at scale.
A program that enables organizations to enhance the places where people spend time.

M11.stem: What does it mean for a company to enroll in the WELL Portfolio?

options.text: Signifies an owner's intent to use the Health-Safety Rating.
Signifies a project managers intent to become a WELL AP .
Signifies portfolio owner's intent to engage in the WELL Portfolio program.
Signifies a buildings intent to be accredited.

M11.stem: How long is the enrollment period for WELL Portfolio?
Can the enrollment period for WELL Portfolio be extended?

options.text: Yes, by an additional six months, up to one year .
No, the enrollment period may not be extended.

M11.stem: What is the minimum commitment for the WELL Portfolio?
What is the role of a portfolio administrator in the WELL Portfolio program?

options.text: To ensure that the project stays on its timeline.
To oversee the program and delegate project responsibilities for the defined portfolio.
To oversee the program process and and coordination of documentation for the defined portfolio.
To oversee the program process and report back to GBCI.

M11.explanation:
The portfolio administrator oversees the WELL Portfolio program process and 
coordination of documentation for the defined portfolio. The portfolio administrator 
is the primary point of contact and will be copied on all correspondence with IWBI 
and the WELL Reviewer.

M11.stem: A defined portfolio must contain at least how many projects, and if the member's total global portfolio contains fewer than this number of projects, what is the minimum then?

options.text: A minimum of 10 projects, unless unless the member’s total global portfolio contains fewer than 10 projects, in which case the minimum project count is two.
A minimum of 20 projects, unless unless the member’s total global portfolio contains fewer than 20 projects, in which case the minimum project count is 10.
A minimum of 8 projects, unless unless the member’s total global portfolio contains fewer than 8 projects, in which case the minimum project count is 4.
A minimum of 4 projects, unless unless the member’s total global portfolio contains fewer than 4 projects, in which case the minimum project count is 2.

M11.explanation:
A defined portfolio must contain at least 10 projects unless the member’s total global 
portfolio contains fewer than 10 projects, in which case the minimum project count 
is two.

M11.stem: What scales of documentation can WELL Portfolio members utilize?

options.text: Photographs, Letters of Assurance, and Project Guidelines.
Individual-scale Documents, Shareable Documents and Guideline Documents.
Streamlined Documents, Modeling Reports and Document Libraries.
There are no scales of documentation.

M11.explanation:
The WELL Portfolio program provides opportunities to submit documentation at 
scale to provide efficiencies in the documentation process. The scales are 
Individual-scale Documents, Shareable Documents and Guideline Documents.

M11.stem: What agency trains and approves WELL Performance Testing Agents for the WELL Portfolio program?
M11.explanation:
A WELL Performance Testing Agent is trained and qualified to conduct performance 
testing for WELL. These agents are trained and approved by GBCI.

M11.stem: During feature review, when can project documents and performance testing results be uploaded for the WELL Portfolio?

options.text: Once all documents have been coordinated.
At the end of the first six months.

M11.explanation:
During feature review, the WELL Reviewer will conduct an examination of 
documentation and performance testing results to confirm compliance with the 
WELL feature requirements. 
Project documents and performance testing results may be uploaded at any time.

M10.stem: According to Part 1: Facilitate Stakeholder Charrette of Feature C02: Integrative Design, when should a stakeholder charrette take place?

options.text: Just after the project has kicked off.

M10.stem: According to Feature C02: Integrative Design Process, who must meet with stakeholders prior to the design and programming of a project trying to achieve a WELL Certification?

options.text: Architects, owner, facility management team and engineers.

M10.stem: Part 2: Promote Health-Oriented Mission of Feature C02: Integrative Design stipulates that the project establishes a health-oriented mission. Where should the health-oriented mission be detailed?

options.text: A general notice to all occupants.

M10.stem: The WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™) Community concept seeks to establish an inclusive, engaged occupant community. What factors link people within a community?

options.text: Engaging in joint action and experiences in shared settings or locations.

M10.explanation: Read the overview for the Community Concept.

M10.stem: What are the Social Determinants of Health?

M04.stem: A new employee has requested more lighting at their workstation. How quickly must the request for supplemental lighting be fulfilled under the WELL Building Standard™ Feature L09: Occupant Lighting Control?
What is the term for excessive brightness of the light source, excessive brightness-contrast and excessive quantity of light?
M04.explanation:
Feature L04: Electric Light Glare defines glare as excessive brightness of the light -source, excessive 
brightness-contrasts and excessive quantity of light and is an integral part of lighting design. Reducing 
glare improves the visual experience of the occupants in the space. Glare has been associated with a 
host of health issues that range from visual discomfort and eye fatigue to headaches and migraines. 
Studies have also shown that glare can lead to visual impairment and discomfort, which can cause 
accidents in the workplace. Individuals under the age of 50 are more sensitive to glare. Since a 
substantial section of the workforce falls into this age group, it is important to address glare to avoid 
visual fatigue and glare-induced headaches.

M08.stem: To comply with Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, a project manager who is overseeing a museum renovation needs to manage exposure risks for asbestos and lead. What other hazardous material must be managed if it is present in the building?

ubiquitous presence still haunts our built and natural environments and can cause 
disease upon exposure.

M05.stem: The Movement Concept states that if physical inactivity were reduced by 25%, approximately how many deaths could be averted globally?
M05.explanation:
The WELL Movement concept aims to promote movement, foster physical activity 
and active living and discourage sedentary behavior, by creating and enhancing 
opportunities through the spaces where we spend our lives. The impact of changing 
the global physical activity narrative is substantial. Worldwide, if physical inactivity 
were reduced by 10%, more than half a million deaths could be averted, while over 
one million deaths could be averted, if physical inactivity was reduced by 25%. 
Furthermore, the elimination of physical inactivity has been predicted to increase the 
global lifespan by an average of 0.68 years.
