a. Cooking demonstrations led by chef-instructors that include fruits and/or 
vegetables, demonstrate cooking skills and integrate hands-on learning 
opportunities. 
b. Nutrition or dietary education sessions led by an accredited dietitian or accredited 
nutritionist. 
c. Individual nutrition consultations led by an accredited dietitian or accredited 
nutritionist. 
d. Gardening or planting workshops focused on edible plants that integrate hands-on 
learning opportunities.

M03.stem: What two options are available for Feature N05: Artificial Ingredients?

options.text: Artificial ingredient phase out or artificial ingredient restriction.
Artificial ingredient phase out or artificial ingredient labeling.
Artificial ingredient labeling or artificial ingredient restriction.
Artificial ingredient restriction or artificial ingredient education.

M03.stem: In Feature N03: Refined Ingredients, whole grains must be present in _____ of sold and provided foods:

options.text: at least 35% of the granola bars.

M03.stem: Reducing sugar content in foods and beverages is a central theme in the Nourishment concept. Standard menu items may not contain more than _______g of sugar per serving.
For projects with commercial dining spaces, at least 50% of available food options are fruits and __________

An insurance company in Flagstaff is planning on providing daily food for their staff. The building will have an eating area on-site, but all food will be prepared off-site. Are the WELL Nourishment requirements applicable here?

options.text: Yes, this concept is required regardless of where food is prepared.
Yes, but only Feature N10: Food Production is applicable.
No, this concept does not have any requirements for outside food or food vendors.
No, this concept only applies for food produced on-site.

M03.stem: What is the intent of Feature N11: Responsible Food Sourcing?

options.text: To ensure that only organic and non-gmo fruits and vegetables are provided to buidling occupants.
To reduce dietary exposure to pesticides, hormones and antibiotics, limit environmental degradation and promote humane livestock practices.
To promote healthier eating choices at work.
To highlight the problems in the industrial food system and look for ways to purchase more locally sustainable foods.

M03.stem: A library in Seattle will be providing one dedicated eating area. In order to fulfill the requirements of Feature N10: Food Production, the dedicated eating area must be provided within what distance of the project boundary?

The WELL Nourishment concept seeks to promote the consumption of fruits and vegetables by increasing availability and accessibility. Around the world, most individuals do not meet the daily recommended ________ servings of fruits and vegetables.
Nutritional Transparency is a critical feature of the WELL Nourishment concept. In commercial dining spaces, what staff positions are required to have annual food allergen training?

M07.stem: In Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones of Feature S01: Sound Mapping, a new office project labels what acoustic zones as part of its sound mapping plan?

options.text: Noisy, Mixed, Equipment and Quiet zones
Noisy, Quiet, Mixed and Circulation zones
Loud, Quiet, Mixed and Circulation zones
Loud, Quiet, Equipment and Circulation zones

M07.explanation:
Loud, Quiet, Mixed and Circulation zones must be mapped. Note: If a loud zone 
directly borders a quiet zone there must be a plan for mitigating the sound 
transmission between the two zones.

M07.stem: A cafeteria, a Category 3 space, is part of the build-out. What is the maximum sound pressure level (SPL) in dBAs for this space if you want to achieve the maximum number of points according to Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature SO2: Maximum Noise Levels.

According to Part 2: Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum Noise Isolation Class (NIC) or Weighted Difference Level (Dw) between rooms for concentration and other regularly occupied spaces?

According to Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what minimums are required for the doors that connect two occupiable rooms and doors to mechanical equipment rooms?

options.text: Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 40 and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 30 and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 35 and seals at the head, jamb and base.

M07.explanation:
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 30, seals at the head, 
jamb and base.

M07.stem: The WELL Sound concept aims to bolster which of the following?
M07.explanation:
The WELL Sound concept aims to bolster occupant health and well-being through the 
identification and mitigation of acoustical comfort parameters that shape occupant 
experiences in the built environment.

M07.stem: In Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels studies have shown exposure to traffic noise can lead to an increased risk of what health issues?
M07.explanation:
Studies have shown that exposure to traffic noise can lead to increased risk of 
cardiovascular system issues, diabetes, hypertension, stroke, depression and high 
blood pressure.

M07.stem: One solution for interior noise as specified in Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels is?

options.text: Selecting HVAC equipment with lower sound ratings.
Providing occupants with noise reducing headphones.

M07.explanation:
Interior noise sources can be controlled by selecting HVAC equipment with lower 
sound ratings and by designing the system to reduce sound within ducts. Exterior 
noise can be controlled by providing sound reduction at the building façade, 
windows and any exterior penetrations.

M07.stem: In order to meet the minimum requirements of Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces of Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces, open workspaces must meet which of the following minimum NRC/aw at ceiling values?

options.text: 0.75 for at least 50% of available ceiling area
0.75 for at least 75% of available ceiling area

M07.explanation:
The Minimum NRC/aw for open workspaces is 0.75 for at least 75% of available 
ceiling area.

M07.stem: Which WELL feature requires projects to strategize an acoustical plan that identifies sources of noise that can negatively impact interior spaces?
M07.explanation:
Feature S01: Sound Mapping requires projects to strategize an acoustical plan that 
identifies sources of noise that can negatively impact interior spaces.

M07.stem: According to Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, to earn 3 Points, what is the maximum Sound Pressure Level (SPL) dBA measurement of an open dining area (a Category 3 space)?
M07.explanation:
Please review Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise 
Levels.

M07.stem: Feature S04: Reverberation Time states that reverberation time is the length of time taken for a sound to decay by __ dB from an initial impulse level.
M07.explanation:
Reverberation time is the length of time taken for a sound to decay by 60 dB from an 
initial impulse level.

M07.stem: A new WELL project is concerned with having the proper sound barriers to protect the privacy of the buildings occupants. If choosing Option 2: Speech privacy in Part 2: Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum NIC + NC or Dw + LAeq for an enclosed office?
M07.explanation:
The sum of the measured Noise Isolation Class (NIC) or Weighted Difference Level 
(Dw) combined with the Noise Criteria Rating (NC) or A-weighted Sound Pressure 
Level (LAeq) within a room for an enclosed Quiet zone which includes enclosed 
offices the minimum NIC + NC or Dw + LAeq is 80.

M07.stem: In Feature S04: Reverberation Time, a solution to reduce reverberation time is?

options.text: Use furniture that is made of wood.
Purchase telecommunication/AV equipment that enhances speech intelligibility.
Add reflective surfaces such as glass or stone.
Add absorptive surface finishes at ceiling and walls.

M07.explanation:
Reverberation time can be controlled by adding absorptive surface finishes at ceilings, 
walls and furniture. Projects that can alter room geometry can change layouts and 
room dimensions to support optimal reverberation times, as needed. Reducing 
reflective surfaces, such as glass, drywall, stone or similar, will also reduce reflected 
sound energy, which increases reverberation time.

M07.stem: To achieve points for Option 1: Reverberation time, design in Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds of Feature S04: Reverberation Time, what is the maximum reverberation time in areas for speech?
A number of studies have indicated that ________________________________ is a major cause of complaint and results in occupant dissatisfaction.

In Part 1: Provide Minimum Background Sound of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound, a sound masking system must be commissioned so that the sound pressure levels (SPL) do not exceed ______ in enclosed rooms labeled as Quiet zones.
M07.explanation:
The sound masking system is commissioned such that the following sound pressure 
levels are not exceeded:

M07.stem: Interior walls must have a minimum Sound Transmission Class (STC) between adjacent Quiet zones per Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature: SO3 Sound Barriers?
M07.explanation:
Interior walls meet the following sound transmission class (STC) or weighted sound 
reduction (Rw) values. If an interior wall meets multiple categories listed, use the 
highest (i.e., more stringent) STC/Rw value listed.

M07.stem: When noise from internal or external sources is increased in a space, occupants have been found to be?

options.text: Easily distracted, hearing impaired and less collaborative.
Easily distracted, less productive and susceptible to burnout.
Less productive, less collaborative and depressed.

M07.explanation:
Architectural trends, such as open workspaces, lightweight construction and 
exposed finishes or HVAC foster acoustically uncomfortable environments. When 
noise from internal or external sources is increased in a space, occupants have been 
found to be easily distracted, less productive and susceptible to burnout. In office 
environments, employees care about privacy and collaboration. In one study from 
the United Kingdom, 99% of employees reported that their concentration was 
impaired by poor acoustical comfort in the workplace. Similar conditions are 
reported in workplaces worldwide. Some reports have shown that occupants are less 
likely to help others under high noise conditions, reducing collaboration in the 
workplace.

M07.stem: A 10-story office building in downtown New York City is aiming for WELL Certification. Due to the location, they are worried about the noise levels from the streets that could travel into the building's interior spaces. To help with this issue, the project team needs to design the floor plans with intent and use in mind. For example, a typical office can be categorized into what four key types of programming?

options.text: Concentration, socialization, teaching and break rooms
Break rooms, teaching, collaboration and socialization

M07.explanation:
To address noise, floor plans should be designed with intent and use in mind. For 
example, a typical office can be categorized into four key types of programming: 
spaces for concentration, collaboration, socialization and learning. Location is 
important since noise from social or collaborative spaces impacts spaces intended for 
concentration or learning.

M07.stem: A suggested solution to exterior noise as specified in Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels is?

options.text: Selecting HVAC equipment with lower sound ratings.
Providing occupants with noise reducing headphones.

M07.explanation:
Interior noise sources can be controlled by selecting HVAC equipment with lower 
sound ratings and by designing the system to reduce sound within ducts. Exterior 
noise can be controlled by providing sound reduction at the building façade, 
windows and any exterior penetrations.

M07.stem: In Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, a new apartment complex must test the background noise levels in the bedrooms to make sure they do not exceed an average of 35 dBA. What minimum period of time must the background noise be measured?

options.text: 6 hour period at any given time of the day
12 hour period that includes the hours from 10 pm to 7 am
Testing must occur for 12 hours between 8 pm to 8 am

M07.explanation:
For dwelling units, an average background noise levels in bedrooms, when measured 
over a 12-hour minimum time period (which must include the hours of 10 pm to 7 
am), should not exceed 35 dBA (Leq).

M07.stem: In Feature S03: Sound Barriers, there is evidence that links noise annoyance in multi-story housing to what health issues?

options.text: Poor mental health and perceived stress

M07.explanation:
Sound that transmits from one room to another through walls or doors can be 
distracting or annoying and also disturb sleep. There is evidence that links noise 
annoyance in multi-story housing to poor mental health and perceived stress in 
residents.

M07.stem: An area mapped as part of the project includes a conference room for collaboration. For this area to comply with Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum STC or RW requirement of the conference room?
M07.explanation:
Interior walls meet the following sound transmission class (STC) or weighted sound 
reduction (Rw) values. If an interior wall meets multiple categories listed, use the 
highest (i.e., more stringent) STC/Rw value listed.

M07.stem: The length of reverberation time is a function of what?

options.text: Sound absorbing properties, room height and number of doors
Room height, surface areas and sound absorbing properties of surface finishes
Room size, surface areas and sound absorbing properties of surface finishes
Room size, surface areas and number of doors

M07.explanation:
Feature S04: Reverberation Time states that the length of reverberation time is a 
function of room size, surface area and sound-absorbing properties of surface 
finishes. Spaces with longer reverberation times may be larger in volume with hard 
surfaces that reflect sound. Spaces with shorter reverberation may be smaller with 
softer surfaces that absorb sound.

M07.stem: Feature S04: Reverberation Time review studies showing high reverberation times in classrooms can link to many negative effects. Which of the following is not one of the many effects?

options.text: Reduces speech intelligibility among hard of hearing a non-native speaking populations

M07.explanation:
Studies have shown that high reverberation times in classrooms increase auditory 
workload in students and reduce cognition, memory retention and concentration. 
Similarly, studies have shown that high reverberation reduces speech intelligibility 
among hard of hearing and non-native speaking populations.

M07.stem: A new learning center is looking for a way to improve the reverberation time within their classrooms. Which of the following options will assist in achieving this goal?

options.text: Reduce reflected sound energy, increased reflective surfaces and add absorptive surfaces.
Add absorptive surfaces, alter room geometry and increase reflective surfaces.
Reduce reflected sound energy, alter room geometry and add absorptive surfaces.
Reduce reflective surfaces, alter room geometry and add absorptive surfaces.

M07.explanation:
Feature S04: Reverberation Time states that Reverberation time can be controlled by 
adding absorptive surface finishes at ceilings, walls and furniture. Projects that can 
alter room geometry can change layouts and room dimensions to support optimal 
reverberation times, as needed. Reducing reflective surfaces, such as glass, drywall, 
stone or similar, will also reduce reflected sound energy, which increases 
reverberation time. Reducing reverberation time also allows audio equipment for 
telecommunication/AV, speech reinforcement or public address to operate with 
higher speech intelligibility performance.

M07.stem: A solution in Feature S02: Sound Reducing Surfaces that minimizes reverberation, significantly reduces sound across human speech frequencies and can greatly reduce unwanted buildup of reflected sound is called?
M07.explanation:
Providing acoustical treatment that significantly reduces sound across human speech 
frequencies can greatly reduce unwanted buildup of reflected sound. Ceilings 
typically provide the greatest area of coverage where highly absorptive materials can 
offer the best performance in reducing reverberation and controlling speech 
intelligibility and occupied noise levels.

M07.stem: What area of a room typically provides the greatest area of coverage where extremely absorptive materials can offer the best outcome to reducing reverberation?
M07.explanation:
Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces states that ceilings typically provide the 
greatest area of coverage where highly absorptive materials can offer the best 
performance in reducing reverberation and controlling speech intelligibility and 
occupied noise levels.

M07.stem: A new project that includes a designated dining hall is looking to reduce sound by adding absorptive surfaces. If they are looking to earn 2 points, what is the minimum NRC/aw for ceilings?

options.text: 0.80 on at least 75% of available ceiling area
0.60 on at least 50% of available ceiling area

M07.explanation:
Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces of Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces 
requires areas for dining be a minimum NRC/aw at ceilings of 0.90 for all available 
ceiling area.

M07.stem: What is the intent of Feature S03: Sound Barriers?

options.text: To increase the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.
To decrease the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.
To increase the level of speech privacy between vertically adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.
To increase the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder thermal comfort.

M07.stem: A WELL project is working on obtaining 1 point for Part 1: Enhanced Speed Reduction Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound. Which features must the project meet in order to earn this point?

options.text: Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds of Feature S04: Reverberation Time
Part 1: Provide Minimum Background of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound
Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones of Feature S01: Sound Mapping
Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers

M07.explanation:
The project achieves the following features and parts: 
a. Achieve two points in either Feature S03 Part 2 - Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls 
or Feature S05 Part 1 – Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces. 
b. Feature S06 Part 1 – Provide Minimum Background Sound.

M07.stem: According to the Feature S07: Impact Noise Management, impact noise travels through what structures?
M07.explanation:
Impact noise travels through structures (e.g., walls, floors, columns, piping) as 
vibrations that are then radiated as airborne noise to a listener

M07.stem: According to Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces of the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™), ceilings provide the ________ area of coverage where highly absorptive materials can offer the best performance in reducing reverberation.
M07.explanation: Read the overview of Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces.

M07.stem: To meet the thresholds for Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating of Feature S07: Impact Noise Management, which ASTM and ISO standards (or equivalent) must be followed?
M07.explanation:
For the following space types, the floor-ceiling construction achieves the following 
Normalized Impact Sound Ratings (NISR), as measured on-site, in accordance with 
ASTM E1007-19, ISO 16283 or equivalent, by a professional in acoustics

M07.stem: A new WELL Certified building that is offering a dining hall for its employees wants to earn the max points for Feature S07: Impact Noise Management for impact noise rating. In order to achieve this, what is the minimum Normalized Impact Sound Ratings (NISR) level needed?
M07.explanation:
Areas for Retail and Dining (If space is within the project boundary) must have a 
minimum NISR of 47 to achieve 2 points.

M07.stem: Depending on the type of surface materials, reverberation time and reflected sound energy has the potential of creating uncomfortable environments. In schools, where spaces need to be conducive to learning, what must the reverberation time support?
M07.explanation: Read overview of Feature S04: Sound Absorption.

M07.stem: According to Part 2: Prioritize Audio Devices and Policies of Feature S07: Enhanced Audio Designs, which of the following does NOT aid in the support of individual acoustical needs?

options.text: Employees must purchase any specialized accessories they will need to utilize sound enhancement technology.
A minimum of one daily quiet hour is scheduled and signage is used to indicate both the location and intended activities of Quiet and Mixed Zones.
All audio devices are managed internally by a qualified professional.
Eligible employees can request alternative workplace arrangements to meet their individual acoustic comfort needs.

M07.explanation:
ALL eligible employees and distance learners are provided telecommunication 
accessories upon request.

M07.stem: The effects of exterior noise from transportation have been shown to impact health and well-being in different ways. What health concern do both men and women share from spending time in spaces that are close to busy highways or airports?
M07.explanation:
Read overview of Sound Concept.  "In one study taken from a sample size of 4,115 
participants, it was found that the risk for myocardial infarction was elevated in men 
from road traffic noise at night and in women by air traffic noise at night."

M07.stem: Improving speech intelligibility in classrooms by implementing audio systems can help support participation for deaf and hard-of-hearing students, which is linked to what areas of improvement, according to WELL Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices?
M07.explanation: Read the overview for Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices.

M07.stem: What does NISR stand for?
M07.explanation: Feature S07: Impact Noise Management.

M07.stem: For all spaces except dwelling units, commercial kitchen spaces, and industrial buildings; category 2 represents what type of area?
M07.explanation:
Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels.  Note: 
Category 1 - Areas for conferencing, learning or speaking.  Category 2 - Enclosed 
areas for concentration,  Category 3 - Open areas for concentration, spaces with PA 
systems, and areas for dining

M07.stem: For dwelling units, what is the maximum average background noise level allowed over a 12-hour period?
M07.explanation:
Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels. 
For dwelling units, the following requirements are met: 
a. Average background noise levels in bedrooms, when measured over a 12-hour 
minimum time period (which 
must include the hours of 10 pm to 7 am), do not exceed 35 dBA

M07.stem: What is the intent of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound?

options.text: To decrease acoustical privacy within and between occupied spaces.
To design spaces with sound reducing surfaces.
To increase acoustical privacy within and between occupied spaces.
To increase the level of sound isolation and speech privacy.

M07.stem: According to Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, exterior noise can be controlled best by?

options.text: Considering at the earliest possible stages of design
Once a noise baseline has been determined.

M07.stem: According to Part 1: Provide Enhanced Speech Intelligibility of Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices, public address systems must be commissioned by an audio engineer in accordance with NFPA 72 (Annex D) or equivalent. What does NFPA stand for?
The human response to mechanical vibrations through a medium is known as
M07.explanation: Read the overview for the Sound Concept.

M07.stem: It is best to consult an audio engineering professional when...

options.text: Preparing to launch a company podcast.
Implementing audio systems to improve speech intelligibility.

M07.explanation:
Read overview for Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices. 
"To provide the best possible outcomes for users, systems should be commissioned 
by a professional in audio engineering in order to meet diverse occupant needs and 
create more accessible spaces."

M07.stem: What feature in the Sound Concept of the WELL Building Standard focuses on achieving desired ambient noise levels?
How many preconditions are there for the WELL Sound Concept?
If your project would like to receive points from Part 2: Provide Enhanced Speed Reduction of Feature SO6: Minimum Background Sound, what other feature or part can be used to achieve points?

options.text: Feature S06 Part 1 – Provide Minimum Background Sound
Achieve one point in Feature S03 Part 2 - Achieve Sound Isolation
Achieve one point in Feature S05 Part 1 – Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces
Achieve two points in Feature S04 Part 1 – Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds

M07.explanation:
Part 2 Provide Enhanced Speech Reduction 
WELL Certification: 1 Pt | WELL Core: 2 Pt 
For All Spaces: 
The project achieves the following features and parts: 
a. Achieve two points in either Feature S03 Part 2 - Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls 
or Feature S05 Part 1 – 
Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces. 
b. Feature S06 Part 1 – Provide Minimum Background Sound. 
Note: Documents submitted for S05 Part 1 during Precertification may be used to 
meet requirements for S06 Part 2.

M06.stem: Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort can be achieved through the use of radiant heating and cooling elements, independent of ventilation systems, in which two space types?
According to the WELL Thermal Comfort concept the human body maintains a stable internal body temperature using what process?
M06.explanation:
Homeothermy is the balancing of heat gains and losses to maintain the bodys core 
temperature within a narrow range.

M06.stem: In order to achieve Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort, what minimum percentage of floor area in all offices and other regularly occupied spaces must meet the requirements set for in the ASHRAE Standard 55-2013 for thermal comfort through the use of hydronic radiant heating and/or cooling systems or along with electric radiant systems?

An open office with large west-facing windows has 29 identical workstations, 10 of which are located next to the exterior windows. According to Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control, which of the following allows occupants to work in a space with their desired temperature?
In the WELL Thermal Comfort concept which of the following is NOT a benefit of radiant temperature systems?
According to the Thermal Comfort concept, how many of the office buildings surveyed in the U.S. provided thermal environments that met generally accepted goals of occupant satisfaction?
M06.explanation: Overview of Feature T02 Enhanced Thermal Performance.

M06.stem: In the WELL Thermal Comfort concept how many primary personal and environmental variables contribute to an occupant's thermal comfort?
M06.explanation: Overview of Feature T01: Thermal Performance.

M06.stem: In the Thermal Comfort concept, which of the following ASHRAE Standard aims to provide a thermal environment, where at least 80% of people will perceive the thermal environment as acceptable.
Which of the following best describes homeothermy from the Thermal Comfort concept?

options.text: Maintaining body core heat through internal means and regulated by the cerebellum.
Stabling of external body temperature regardless of internal influence and regulated by the amygdala.
The balancing of heat gains and losses to maintain the body's core temperature within its narrow range, 36-38 degrees C [97-100 degrees F], and regulated by the hypothalamus.
Controlling body core temperature through internal means and regulated by the thalamus.

M06.stem: To achieve Part 1: Survey for Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort, a post-occupancy survey should be administered how many times per year and in what months?

options.text: Once at the beginning of the year.
At least twice a year, including once in June, July or August and once in December, January or February, at least six months after occupancy.
3 times per year in January, April and August.
4 times per year, once in each fiscal quarter.

M06.explanation:
A post-occupancy survey should be administered at least twice a year, including once 
in June, July or August and once in 
December, January or February, at least six months after occupancy.

M06.stem: Salesforce just occupied new office space in Chicago, Illinois. The office has 900 occupants that regularly report to this location. What is the minimum response rate needed for the surveys conducted at least twice a year for Part 1: Survey for Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort?
If a project is able to exceed the thermal comfort satisfaction thresholds by 90% or more, how many points is the project awarded for Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort?
Projects seeking Feature T03: Thermal Zoning compliance should provide control over temperature in the space through thermostat being present in the thermal zone , or...

options.text: Supplied personal Fans and Space Heaters
A lax dress-code and company-issued coats and blankets.
Work stations being located near operable windows
A digital interface accessible to occupants on a computer or phone

M06.stem: With the exception of high-humidity areas, the mechanical system has the capability of maintaining relative humidity at all times by what methods covered in Feature T07: Humidity Control.

options.text: Adding or removing moisture from the air.

M06.stem: According to Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort, at least 50% of what areas must be shaded for more than half of the daylight hours each day by tree canopies, awnings, or other structures?

options.text: Plazas, seating areas and other outdoor areas of congregation

M06.stem: Projects seeking points in Part 1: Provide Windows with Multiple Opening Modes of Feature T08 B: Enhanced Operable Windows will provide instructions for window operation through signage or other communications that indicate which of the following:

options.text: Windows are not to be opened when mechanical cooling is in operation (not required if no mechanical cooling is present or if mechanical cooling system is configured to disengage automatically when windows are open).
Windows with high openings (if present) are to be used in cold weather.
Windows with low openings are to be used during mild and/or warm weather.

M06.explanation:
Instructions for window operation are provided through signage or other 
communications to regular building 
occupants to indicate the following: 
a. Windows with low openings are to be used during mild and/or warm weather. 
b. Windows are not to be opened when mechanical cooling is in operation (not 
required if no mechanical cooling is 
present or if mechanical cooling system is configured to disengage automatically 
when windows are open). 
c. Windows with high openings (if present) are to be used in cold weather.

M06.stem: For Commercial Kitchen Spaces, the maximum operative temperature cannot exceed what degrees F° (C° ) per Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Comfort of Feature T01: Thermal Performance?
M06.explanation:
For Commercial Kitchen Spaces, the operative temperature in the kitchen cannot 
exceed 80 ° F(27 ° C).

M06.stem: In Part 2: Achieve Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Enhanced Thermal Performance, a post-occupancy survey is administered twice a year. What specific months must the surveys be administered?

options.text: March, April or May and December, January or February
June, July or August and December, January or February
June, July or August and March, April, or May

M06.stem: With Feature T01: Thermal Performance projects, when conducting ongoing monitoring, the number and location of sampling points must comply with the requirements outlined where?

For Feature T01: Thermal Performance compliance, if a project's measurements are undertaken by a WELL Performance Testing Agent in compliance with the Performance Verification Guidebook, where are the results submitted to for recertification purposes?
When seeking Feature T03: Thermal Zoning compliance, the maximum size of each thermal zone is:

options.text: 650 ft² or 10 occupants, whichever is larger
750 ft² or 10 occupants, whichever is larger
850 ft² or 20 occupants, whichever is larger
650 ft² or 5 occupants, whichever is larger

M06.stem: Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort requires projects:

options.text: To use independently controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and privately controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and independently controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and privately controlled ventilation systems.

M06.stem: Which WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to check thermal comfort parameters in their buildings that can be used as feedback for building managers and users to take appropriate actions.
This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to design outdoor spaces to avoid excessive wind and manage elevated temperatures through shading or other strategies.
In Part 1: Manage Relative Humidity of Feature T07: Humidity Control , the mechanical system must have the capability of maintaining relative humidity in what range?
When determining occupant satisfaction, this WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects provide a high level of thermal comfort?

This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to create indoor thermal environments that provide comfortable thermal conditions to the majority of people in support of their health, well-being and productivity.

In Part 1: Manage Outdoor Heat of Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort at least ___ of pedestrian pathways and building entrances must be shaded for more than half of the daylight hours each day by tree canopies, awnings, or other structures.
For Feature T08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows compliance, projects must design windows with:

options.text: Controls for window operation are positioned higher than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned less than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned not more than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.

M06.stem: For a project to achieve Feature 01: Thermal Performance, there are two options to choose from: Annual testing and Continuous Monitoring. If the project chooses Annual testing, what verification method is required to be submitted to IWBI?

A project team is anxious to complete the project and earn their WELL Certification. For Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort, what time period must the project team wait to conduct the survey of tenants?

options.text: At end of first year of occupancy.

M06.stem: The intent of Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control is to maximize and personalize thermal comfort among all individuals. Personal Cooling Options for all regular occupants may include:

options.text: A cooling mat for their feet.

M06.stem: A law firm in Cincinnati would like to allow for thermal zones in their open office space. How can this company earn the most points for Feature T03: Thermal Zoning?

options.text: By providing one zone per 10 occupants.
By providing one zone per 200 ft2.
By providing one zone per 2 occupants.
By providing one zone per 5 occupants.

M06.stem: Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment of Feature T01: Thermal Performance has a verification method requirement for submitting materials to IWBI. What is the verification method for this option?

options.text: Letter of Assurance - Engineer, Performance Test, Technical Document

M06.stem: Humidity plays a big role in indoor air quality and keeping occupants healthy inside of buildings. In order to manage long-term humidity data for Feature T07: Humidity Control, what relative humidity is required for at least the pervious six months?

options.text: Between 40% and 60% for at least 98% of all business hours of the year .
Between 30% and 60% for at least 98% of all business hours of the year .
Between 25% and 55% for at least 93% of all business hours of the year .
Between 30% and 60% for at least 92% of all business hours of the year .

M06.stem: As part of Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control, occupants can warm their individual environments. What is an example of an approved personal heating option?

For performance verified environmental conditions in Feature T01: Thermal Performance, what is one of the PMV ranges of the thermal comfort conditions for mechanically conditioned regularly occupied spaces?
How many preconditions are there for the Thermal Comfort Concept?
What other features must Feature T09: Outdoor Thermal Comfort part 3 Support Outdoor Nature Access achieve to satisfy the feature requirement?

options.text: Part 1: Manage Outdoor Heat or Part 2: Avoid Excessive Wind of Feature T09: β Outdoor Thermal Comfort and Part 2: Provide Nature Access Outdoors of Feature M09: Enhanced Access to Nature.
Feature T07: Humidity Control and Feature T09: Enhanced Operable Windows
Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment and Part 2: Monitor Thermal Parameters of Feature T01: Thermal Performance.

M06.stem: According to the overview of the Thermal Comfort concept in the WELL Building StandardTM, what percentage of office workers have expressed dissatisfaction with their thermal environment?
Thermal comfort greatly influences our experiences in the places we live and work and is one of the highest contributing factors including__________?
M06.explanation: Located in the Overview section of the Thermal Comfort concept.

M06.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to increase thermal control of the space, by allowing control of either the conditions of a thermal zone or movement between thermal zones.

This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to improve thermal comfort of people in the space through the provision of personal thermal comfort devices and flexible dress codes that support individual thermal preferences.
This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to maintain optimum relative humidity levels that are conducive to human health and well-being.
FeatureT08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows requires windows that can be opened at different elevations to:

options.text: Restrict air flow at different outdoor temperaturs
Maintain air flow at different outdoor temperatures
Provide desired air flow at different outdoor temperatures
Provide limited air flow at different outdoor temperatures

M06.stem: According to Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort, heat island effects can greatly impact outdoor temperatures and make outdoor spaces too warm for building occupants to enjoy. Adverse health effects include heat-related illness, fatigue and increased heart rate. What is an example of a mitigation strategy to improve outdoor thermal comfort in a heat island?

options.text: Adding more structures, like metal sculptures.
Using sand to cover areas of concrete.

M06.stem: According to Feature T06: Thermal Comfort Monitoring, what can cause the buildings occupants to experience itchy eyes, headache and throat irritation?

options.text: Cold temperatures, dry air, poor ventilation.
High levels of heat, humidity, poor ventilation.
High levels of outdoor pollen, poor ventilations, unfavorable outdoor weather.

M06.stem: Outdoor weather, indoor occupancy and building physics and performance all have a direct impact on human perceptions of what WELL Concept?
M06.explanation:
Outdoor weather, indoor occupancy and building physics and performance, including 
ventilation rates, are highly variable and have a direct impact on human perceptions 
of thermal comfort.

M06.stem: A new office building in San Diego is being designed for optimal Thermal Comfort for it's employees. To give employee's a little more control of their environment the building is adding operable windows. According to Part 1: Provide Windows with Multiple Opening modes of Feature T08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows, what percent of operable windows may be opened so that at least half of the opening is not more than 5.9 ft above the finished floor and opening is at least 1 ft in the smallest dimension?
M06.explanation:
At least 70% of operable windows may be opened such that at least half of the 
opening is not more than 5.9 ft above the finished floor and opening is at least 1 ft in 
the smallest dimension. At least one such window is present in each room with 
operable windows.

M06.stem: According to Part 1: Provide Thermostat Control of Feature T03: Thermal Zoning, for optimal performance, temperature sensors should be placed at least how many feet away from exterior walls, windows and doors, direct sunlight, air supply diffusers, mechanical fans, and heaters?
M06.explanation:
Temperature sensors are positioned at least 3.3 ft away from exterior walls, windows 
and doors, direct sunlight, air supply diffusers, mechanical fans, heaters or any other 
significant sources of heat or cold.

M02.stem: Nearly two-thirds of the human body is composed of water and a good majority of the global population is under hydrated. Depending on age, sex and pregnancy status, what are the recommended values of daily water consumption by adults?
M02.explanation: Review the Water Concept, Overview.

M02.stem: The project team for an existing building is pursuing WELL Certification. All water being delivered to the project has been tested, and the turbidity level was found to be less than 1.0 NTU. What other test must be passed to achieve Feature W01: Water Quality Indicators?

options.text: The pH is level is a neutral level of 7
Quarterly testing for inorganic metals is done
An active carbon filter is installed on all water meant for human contact
Total coliforms are not detected in any 100 ml sample

M02.stem: Which of the following building systems deal with turbidity limits?

A tenant buildout project in Sarasota Florida is concerned with the water quality in the building. After testing some of the water they discovered the water had a salty taste. What chemical can cause this?
M02.explanation: Refer to Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality.

M02.stem: According to Part 1: Verify Water Quality Indicators of Feature W01: Water Quality Indicators, water delivered to the project and intended for human contact must not exceed what maximum thresholds for nephelometric turbidity?
M02.explanation:
Water delivered to the project and intended for human contact (e.g., drinking, 
cooking and dishwashing, handwashing, showering, or bathing) meets the following 
thresholds: 
a. Turbidity is less than or equal to 1.0 NTU, FTU, or FNU (nephelometric turbidity, 
formazin turbidity, or formazin nephelometric units, respectively). 
b. Coliforms are not detected in any 100 ml sample.

M02.stem: According to Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality, all water being delivered to the project area for human consumption must meet which of the following maximum limit for Iron?
M02.explanation: Refer to Part 1 of Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality.

M02.stem: Many people are inadequately hydrated, and often reach for sugar-sweetened beverages instead of water because of what reason?

options.text: You do not need to drink a lot of water.
Mistrust in the safety of drinking water.

M02.explanation:
Even where safe water is usually at the tap, many people often mistrust the safety of 
their water.

M02.stem: While there have been drastic improvements in the drinking water quality over the years, there has been an increasing risk from industrial, agricultural, and pharmaceutical sources. According to the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™), what contaminants derived from this issue can lead to impaired oxygen transport in infants and impair neurodevelopment in children?
M02.explanation: Review the Water Concept, Overview

M02.stem: In order to meet the requirements of Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Organics and Pesticides of Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality, what two options are available to the project for this precondition?

options.text: Drinking water quality report or on-site testing.

M02.stem: In Part 1: Assess and Maintain Drinking Water Quality of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management, how often should the drinking water be tested in order to assess and maintain drinking water quality?
M02.explanation: Located in Part 1 of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management.

M02.stem: How often are water testing results submitted to the WELL digital platform to satisfy Part 1: Assess and Maintain Drinking Water Quality of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management?
M02.explanation:
This WELL feature requires pre-testing of water quality parameters to determine 
treatment needs, monitoring at a more frequent interval, and disclosure of water 
results. All test results are submitted annually through the WELL digital platform.

M02.stem: Legionnaire’s disease especially affects which of the following:
M02.explanation: Read the Overview for Feature W03: Basic Water Management.

M02.stem: In Part 1: Assess and Maintain Drinking Water Quality for Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management, for all spaces, what contaminates must be tested for quarterly at the point of dispensing?
M02.explanation:
Found in Part 1: Assess and Maintain Drinking Water Quality of Feature W05: 
Drinking Water Quality Management.

M02.stem: A project in Miami, Florida is working on completing the requirements for Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality. In Part 1: Meet Chemical Thresholds, what parameters do all drinking water dispensers meet?
According to WELL, building-scale interventions can improve water quality depending on the contaminants that need to be removed. Which system is not able to capture water contaminants?
M02.explanation: Found in Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality overview.

M02.stem: Lead, arsenic, antimony, mercury, nickel, copper, cadmium, and Chromium are all examples of which of the following?
M02.explanation: Read overview to Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality.

M02.stem: To promote proper hydration, what is the maximum distance all parts of regularly occupied floor space should be from water dispensers in Feature W06: Drinking Water Promotion?
M02.explanation:
At least one drinking water dispenser (minimum one per floor) is located within 30 m 
[100 ft] walk distance of all regularly occupied floor area and in all dining areas.

M02.stem: As part of dispenser maintenance for Feature W06: Drinking Water Promotion, how often should the mouthpieces/outlets, protective guards and basins of drinking water fountains and dispensers be cleaned?
M02.explanation: Found in W06: Drinking Water Promotion.

M02.stem: A software company is building a new 100,000 sq ft office building in San Francisco. The client is concerned with microbial growth in the building's internal water supply. Which of the following filtration system would help prevent microbial growth in the potable water according to Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality?

options.text: Reverse osmosis system with a carbon filter

M02.stem: What bathroom accommodations can be implemented within a business office to help support hygiene in order to meet the requirements of Part 1: Provide Bathroom and Handwashing Accommodations of Feature W08: Hygiene Support?

options.text: Install a hook or shelf for storage support
Provide access to menstrual products at no cost
Include trash cans in womens and single user bathrooms

M02.stem: A business office provides an onsite daycare facility for working parents. They include access to family bathrooms for those employees. What would the bathroom need to include in Part 1: Provide Bathroom and Handwashing Accommodations for Feature W08: Hygiene Support?

options.text: Children's Sinks and toilets, or accommodation to use the adult size options.
Stalls just for children to use.

M02.explanation:
Family bathrooms must contain the following: 
 
 Changing table for infants. 
 Children’s toilet facilities or accommodations for child use of adult size toilet. 
 Children’s sinks or accommodations for child use of adult size sink 
 Motion sensor lights. 
 Skid resistant floors. 
 Safety grab bars. 
 At least one designated location for bags

M02.stem: A new construction building in a rainy climate is aiming for WELL certification. Excess moisture and dampness in a building is often a problem in this area. This can lead to health problems for the building's occupants. According to Part 1: Design Envelope for Moisture Protection of Feature W07: Moisture Management, which of the following is not a strategy that must be implemented to prevent moisture?

options.text: Management of site drainage and storm water during construction.
Install porous mats at all entry ways to prevent tracking in water.

M02.explanation:
This is not a method for moisture protection listed in Part 1: Design Envelope for 
Moisture Protection of Feature W07: Moisture Management.

M02.stem: Feature W07: Moisture Management of the WELL Building Standard stipulates that in order to control the moisture of the interior of a building you must put into place a plan that addresses the following:

options.text: The replacement of moisture-sensitive building materials.
Condensation on cold surfaces, such as basements, slab-on-grade floors, the inside of exterior walls and glazing.
Putting a water drainage plan into place.

M02.explanation:
Along protection of moisture-sensitive building materials and selection of 
moisture-resistant materials, you must implement measures to address the 
condensation on cold surfaces such as basements, slab-on-grade floors, the inside of 
exterior walls and glazing.

M02.stem: A WELL project is implementing a Mold and Moisture Management Plan. Which of the following would not be found on this plan in Part 3: Implement Mold and Moisture Management Plan of Feature W07: Moisture Management?

options.text: Submit quarterly mold and leaks inspection report.
Implement a system to periodically check for water pipe leaks.
Create system for occupants and tenants to report mold or water damage.
Create a schedule to inspect for water damage or pooling, discoloration and mold on ceilings, walls, floors and HVAC equipment.

M02.explanation: Reports only need to be submitted annually through the WELL digital platform.

M02.stem: A new office complex wants to help reduce demand on its city's water supply by installing a non-potable water capture and reuse system. However, they are worried about cross-contamination. In Feature W09 β: Onside Non -Potable Water Reuse, what does not need to be included in the safety plan?

options.text: A list of all applicable local codes and regulation for where the system is being installed.
A blueprint of the building where the system is being installed.
A list of the key team roles for design, operations, maintenance and third party inspections.
A flow diagram that displays the non-potable water sources, conveyances, storage units, treatment devices and points of use.

M02.explanation: This is not one of the requirements listed for the safety plan.

M02.stem: Feature W09 β: Onsite Non-Potable Water Reuse of the WELL Building Standard states that a project can help decrease water use by what percent by installing non-potable water reuse and capture systems?
M02.explanation:
By properly managing and controlling non-potable water reuse and capture systems, 
projects can help maintain occupant health and safety, while contributing to 
decreased water use by up to 75% in new buildings.

M02.stem: To help support effective handwashing practice in a building for Part 3: Support Effective Handwashing of Feature W08: Hygiene Support, what is one requirement that must be addressed?

options.text: Proper signage is displayed for locations of handwashing stations.
The faucet is designed for water to flow directly down the drain.
The water column is at minimum 3 inches away from the edge of the sink.

M02.stem: According to the WELL Building Standard Feature W07: Moisture Management overview, what percentage of buildings are affected by excess moisture and dampness in Europe, Canada, and the US?
M02.explanation: 20% of buildings in Europe, Canada, and the U.S deal will excess moisture.

M02.stem: The Water concept in the WELL Building Standard does NOT aim to improve which of the following?

options.text: Increase proper hydration in the building occupants.
Reduce health risk associated with contaminated water and excessive moisture in buildings.
Provide adequate sanitation through better infrastructure design.
Provide access to better water quality by promoting bottle water.

M02.explanation: This is not part of the Water Feature.

M02.stem: Access to good quality water not only helps with sanitation and hygiene but it can also reduce the risk of acquiring what kind of diseases?
M02.explanation: Read the Water concept overview in the WELL Building Standard.

M02.stem: A project team is working to earn the optimization Part 3: Support Effecting Handwashing of Feature W08: Hygiene support which aims to help improve the surrounding environment of handwashing areas reduce the risk of bacteria developing. Which of the following would help aid in this?

options.text: Have the handwashing sinks be separate from the bathrooms.
Have a faucet design that prevents water from flowing directly into the drain.
Continuously top off the soap dispensers to prevent it from running out.

M02.explanation:
A sink drain stopper could also be installed as a strategy here. 
Feature W08: Hygiene Support overview states that "Water splashing from the drain 
may spread bacteria to surrounding areas."

M02.stem: A new WELL project is going to have an onsite cafeteria for its employees. In order to meet the requirements in Part 1: Provide Bathroom and Handwashing Accommodations of Feature W08: Hygiene Support for proper handwashing accommodations for this commercial kitchen and dining space, what must be done?

options.text: Have handwashing stations at every entrance.
Have 5 or more handwashing stations available.
Post clear signage to the nearest handwashing location at the entrance to all food prep and consumption areas.
There are no special requirements for commercial spaces.

M02.stem: To ensure the drinking water of a building is within the threshold for organics and pesticides, routine on-site testing can be done, according to Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Organics and Pesticides of Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality. What is a requirement that must be met?

options.text: Drinking water dispensers provide water that meets the threshold for at least two pesticides and three organic contaminants.
The test must show no traces of any pesticides or organic contaminants.
Any pesticides or organic contaminants are okay as long as they are under the threshold.
Tested water shows no trace of either pesticides OR organic contaminants.

M02.stem: Part 1: Meet Chemical Thresholds of Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality of Feature W02: Drinking Water Quality stipulates that a Project must provide at least one drinking water dispenser plus how many water dispensers per dwelling unit?

Legionella bacteria naturally occur in water at low concentrations but may colonize in recirculating water systems. If contaminated water aerosols are inhaled it can lead to many health issues and even death. In Part 2: Implement Legionella Management Plan of Feature W03: Basic Water Management, in order to prevent the colonization of Legionella, a proper management plan must be put into place. Which of the following isn't included in the items that need to be addressed?

A Legionella Management Plan must document monitoring results, corrective actions, and any sample results and submit them through the WELL digital platform. As part of Part 2: Implement Legionella Management Plan of Feature W03: Basic Water Management, how often must projects submit this documentation?
M02.explanation: Documentation only needs to be submitted once a year.

M02.stem: A new office project is not only concerned about the health-based concerns in the drinking water, but with the water aesthetics as well. What filtration system can help with the taste and visual appearances of the building drinking water, as found in Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality?
M02.explanation:
Read the overview for Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality.  Treatment options 
include filtration with carbon media and reverse osmosis.

M02.stem: How is drinking water taste verified, in Feature W04: Enhanced Water Quality?

A 2-story first-time registered project is testing the drinking water quality in its building. Where does Part 1: Assess and Maintain Drinking Water Quality of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management state is the best place to take the sample from?

options.text: From the water dispenser that is closest to the water source.
From every water dispenser on a single floor.
From the water dispenser that is farthest away from the water source.
All drinking water dispensers should be tested.

M02.explanation:
In addition to the closest water dispenser, for building more than 2 floors, samples 
should be taken from the highest floor, and the water dispenser located the farthest 
from the source. If the building is 12 or more stories, samples need to be taken from 
one additional drinking water dispenser for every 10 floors.

M02.stem: A new WELL project is concerned with being transparent with the tenants about the building's drinking water quality. In order to comply with Part 2: Promote Drinking Water Transparency of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management, a couple of things must prominently be displayed near the sources of drinking. One of those items is the most recent test results for water quality, which includes the date and compliance with thresholds. What is the other item that must be posted?

options.text: The last date of maintenance for the drinking dispenser.
What chemicals are being used to enhance the drinking water.
Treatment technologies of the filters used, plus the recent date of maintenance or replacement.
The cleaning scheduled for the water drinking dispenser.

M02.stem: Feature W06: Drinking Water Promotion details dispenser availability and dispenser maintenance. According to this feature, which option does not aid in drinking water promotion?

options.text: Newly installed water fountains are designed for water bottle refilling.
The water that supplies the dispensers is piped through the buildings water supply or stored in refillable containers.
At least one water dispenser is located within a 100 ft walking distance from all regularly occupied floor and dining areas.
Post the cleaning schedules of the water dispensers within view when standing at the dispenser.

M02.explanation: This is not one of the methods to help promote drinking water.

M02.stem: The WELL Building Standard seeks to manage water quality in buildings. As part of this, Feature W07: Moisture Management details the issue of excess moisture and dampness in buildings. How many of the asthma cases in the U.S. are linked to excess moisture and dampness in a building?

A 10-story new office complex in New Orleans, LA is working on the interior of the building. Since New Orleans receives a large amount of annual rainfall, the project team is taking measures to protect against any moisture and mold issues. In Part 2: Design Interiors for Moisture Management of Feature W07: Moisture Management, besides addressing condensation issues, what is another issue that needs to be addressed?

options.text: Proper drainage for moisture build up.

M02.explanation:
All hard piped fixtures must have a readily accessible single-throw manual shut off or 
automatically shut off at the point of connection. Also, all water treatment devices 
must have a waste line fixed in place with a backflow preventer.

M02.stem: A project designer is focusing their efforts on creating a bathroom design that will accommodate for enhanced hygiene support. Which of the following features would not aid in their design according to Part 2: Enhance Bathroom Accommodations of Feature W08: Hygiene Support?

options.text: Providing single use hand drying cloths that can be washed and reused.
A method to exit the bathroom hands free.

M02.explanation:
This is not one of the methods mentioned. Having a contactless hand dryer would be 
a better option.

M02.stem: What is required to be submitted annually through the WELL digital platform for a safety plan for Non-Potable water capture and reuse?

options.text: Documents of start up procedure, maintenance logs, results from testing.
List of key team members, maintenance logs, emergency operations.
Testing results, maintenance logs, chemicals used to treat odors.
Emergency operations, a description of the system, maintenance logs.

M02.explanation:
Projects submit annually through the WELL digital platform documentation of the 
startup procedure, maintenance logs, results from verification tests (when 
applicable), and third-party inspections.

M02.stem: When testing the water quality, what temperature should the water be when tested?
M02.explanation:
Sample water should be taken from the cold water fixture. However, if conditions 
exist preventing adjustment of the water temperature, perform the testing at the 
temperature of the water provided and make note of conditions.

M02.stem: A WELL Certified office building is testing the building's water supply for levels of disinfectants. In order to obtain a proper sample, how long must the water be running before gathering a sample?
M02.explanation:
Run the water for at least 30 seconds before gathering a water sample (unless the 
sample is drawn immediately following a previous sample).

M02.stem: What type of verification is needed for Part 1: Design Envelope for Moisture Protection of Feature W07: Moisture Management?
A building is using reclaimed water for the urinals and toilets through the building's bathrooms. How often does the reclaimed water quality need to be tested?
M02.explanation:
Water is used in situations that do not lead to human contact are not required to 
meet the water quality requirements.

M02.stem: How frequently in the building does signage indicating the water quality results need to be placed according to Part 2: Promote Drinking Water Transparency of Feature W05: Drinking Water Quality Management?

options.text: The amount is at the discretion of the project team.
At the drinking fountains close to every exit/entrance.

M02.explanation:
The project team is encouraged to consider how to most effectively inform the 
occupants of the water quality results when determining the placement and density 
of such displays.
